PUBLIC REPORTED DOWNED TREES AND TREE LIMBS IN ORANGE. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND SPORADIC STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE JUST EAST OF RAGLEY ALONG LONNIE COLE ROAD. A LARGE TREE BRANCH FELL ON A MOBILE HOME AT 144 LONNIE COLE RD CAUSING SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE. A PINE TREE WAS SNAPPED AT ITS BASE AND FELL ACROSS LONNIE COLE RD. STRAIGHT LINE WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT BETWEEN 60 AND 70 MPH.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND SPORADIC STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE IN GILLIS IN A NEIGHBORHOOD JUST EAST OF HWY 171 OFF OF TOPSY RD. A FEW 3-4 INCH DIAMETER TREE BRANCES WERE SNAPPED BUT STILL HANGING ON TREES ALONG WITH NUMEROUS SMALL BRANCHES SCATTERED ON THE GROUND. NO PROPERTY DAMAGE WAS NOTED. STRAIGHT LINE WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT NEAR 60 MPH.
PUBLIC POSTED PHOTO OF TREE THAT FELL INTO A HOUSE. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

AN EF1 TORNADO LIFTED THE ROOF OFF A HOUSE AND IT LANDED ON THREE NEIGHBORING HOUSES AND VEHICLES TO THE EAST. ALTHOUGH PEOPLE WERE HOME AT THE TIME...NO INJURIES WERE REPORTED. AN OAK TREE WAS SNAPPED ON THE STREET ALSO.

THE EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN SOUTHEAST OF WOODVILLE NEAR HIGHWAY 287 WHERE TREE FALL DISPLAYED GREATER CONVERGENCE AND SOME ROOFING MATERIAL WAS REMOVED FROM A BUILDING. THE TORNADO CONTINUED TO THE SOUTHEAST SNAPPING NUMEROUS SOFTWOOD TREES BEFORE DISSIPATING JUSTEAST OF CR 4250.

THE EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF FM 1005 NORTH OF MAGNOLIA SPRINGS WHERE IT UPROOTED AND SNAPPED SEVERAL TREES. A HOME ALONG FM 1005 WAS DAMAGED WHEN A TREE FELL ONTO IT.THE TORNADO CONTINUED TO THE NORTHEAST AND CAUSED TREE DAMAGE BEFORE DISSIPATING NEAR BOGGY CREEK NORTH OF CR 317.

*** 3 INJ *** A TORNADO HAS BEEN COMFIRMED ALONG HIGHWAY 96 NEAR CALL. THE NWS EMPLOYEE AT THE SCENE INDICATES THAT THE TORNADO REACHED AN INTENSITY OF EF2 WITH WINDS AROUND 120 MPH AT ITS PEAK. A MOBILE HOME WAS ROLLED WITH TIE DOWNS PULLED FROM THE GROUND. 3 PEOPLE WERE INSIDE AND ALL ESCAPED WITH MINOR INJURIES. MANY TREES WERE ALSO DAMAGED. THE SURVEY IS STILL ONGOING AN MORE INFO WILL FOLLOW WITH A PNS LATER.

RADAR IMAGERY INDICATED A TORNADO DEBRIS SIGNITURE AROUND BAYOU JACK ROAD AND JIMS CAMP ROAD THIS MORNING. KALB METEOROLOGIST ADAM BURNISTON WENT TO THE SITE AND SENT IN PICTURES OF TREE DAMAGE AND ROOFING MATERIAL REMOVED FROM A SHED NEAR BAYOU JACK CHURCH WHERE THE TDS WAS INDICATED. THE TORNADO THEN MOVED INTO THE FOREST IN NORTHEAST SAINT LANDRY PARISH.
